free_text: Neuton scattering cross sections for Te-126 and Te-130
nuclei with energies 1.5 - 5.0 MeV
Sov.At.Energy - translation from AE
data for integral cross section
are given.
for 1-3 MeV
for 5-7 MeV
Neutron spread 60-100 keV for energies 1-3 MeV and
90-150 keV for energies 5-7 MeV is mainly due to target
thickness and finite size of sample
Te isotopes metal powder(enriched to 98.4 and 99.3%
for Te-126 and Te-130)
Flight path 2.5-2.7 m
Electrostatic Accelerator EG-5
Scintillation detector - stilbene with diameter
50 mm and thickness 50 mm
following corrections were made:
* on anisotropy in yield of neutrons from target,
* beam attenuation in sample,
* multiple scattering and angular discrimination.
Total uncertainty included all experimental
uncertainties:
* statistics of measurements and handling,
* uncertainty in normalization;
* uncertainties of used oxygen cross sections
Data were received from Dr.I.A.Korzh
UkrNDC
Data correspond to fig.1 from AE,67,(2),147,1989
